commit.message:
fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix to prevent duplicate revision errors (#120)

* fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix to prevent duplicate revision errors

Add a data source to read existing container app state and compute whether
the revision suffix has actually changed. Only send the suffix via
azapi_update_resource when the user explicitly changes it, avoiding the
'revision with suffix already exists' error on updates.

- Add data.azapi_resource.existing to read current revision suffix
- Add locals for existing_revision_suffix and revision_suffix_to_send
- Add terraform_data.revision_suffix to track suffix changes
- Add azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix for conditional suffix updates
- Add examples/revision_suffix/ with test scenarios

Fixes #115

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add HasChange guard for revision_suffix using data source + azapi_update_resource

Prevents 'revision with suffix already exists' error on subsequent applies
by separating revision suffix updates from the main resource PUT body.

Changes:
- Add data.azapi_resource.existing to read current state from Azure
- Add locals for existing_revision_suffix and revision_suffix_to_send
  (HasChange guard logic moved from locals.tf to main.tf)
- Add terraform_data.revision_suffix tracking var.template.revision_suffix
- Add azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix with replace_triggered_by
- Remove locals from locals.tf (now co-located with data source in main.tf)

* refactor: rename terraform_data and azapi_update_resource to more generic names

- terraform_data.revision_suffix -> terraform_data.update_keeper
- azapi_update_resource.revision_suffix -> azapi_update_resource.post_creation_update

* fix lint issue

* fix lint issue

* fix lint issue

* remove unused provider

* fix: use PATCH for template updates to prevent revision suffix and secrets errors

Main module changes:
- Extract template body into local.template_body to share between
  azapi_resource and azapi_resource_action
- Add ignore_changes for entire body.properties.template on the main
  azapi_resource to prevent PUT from re-sending revision-scoped properties
- Use azapi_resource_action with PATCH method instead of azapi_update_resource
  (which uses PUT and overwrites secrets) for post-creation template updates
- Track var.template changes via terraform_data.update_keeper to trigger
  replacement of azapi_resource_action.post_creation_update only when
  template properties actually change
- Use local.resource_group_id for data.azapi_resource.existing parent_id
  to avoid unnecessary data source reads when resource_group_id is provided

Example fixes:
- Add resource_group_id and location to all module calls across examples
  to ensure data.azapi_resource.existing can resolve at plan time
- Add subnet delegation for Microsoft.App/environments in vnet and acr examples
- Add ignore_changes for infrastructure_resource_group_name and
  workload_profile on container_app_environment in vnet and acr examples
- Split acr example into two subnets: one for private endpoints,
  one with delegation for container app environment

* chore(examples): register Microsoft.App resource provider via azapi_resource_action

Not all test subscriptions have Microsoft.App registered. Add
azapi_resource_action to register the provider in every example,
and ensure azurerm_container_app_environment depends_on it.

Also add azapi provider version constraint (~> 2.5) to examples
that were missing it.

* feat: add retry on azapi_resource_action for active provisioning conflict

* fix: use attribute syntax for retry block on azapi_resource_action

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: make Entra auth conditional and load appsettings from assembly dir (0.7.1)

When running as a dotnet tool, appsettings.json was not loaded from the
assembly directory because ContentRootPath defaulted to CWD. Additionally,
AddMicrosoftIdentityWebApi crashed with IDW10106 when no AzureAd:ClientId
was configured. Auth registration is now conditional on ClientId presence,
enabling a clean local-only mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refine Makimono: fixed header, lighter text, tighter layout

- Split into fixed title header + scrollable content below
- Use explicit light colors (#e8ddd0, #b0a494, #c9a96e) instead
  of CSS vars designed for light backgrounds — now readable against
  the dark overlay
- Tighten panel padding (48px → 20px), remove min-height per panel
- Bump font sizes: philosophy 15px, step desc 14px, subtitle 14px
- Splash mode hides scroll, centers header only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: AES transpose bug and stale test vectors

- Remove row/column transpose in aes_encrypt_block and aes_decrypt_block;
  AES state is stored column-major so load/store is a straight memcpy
- Drop aes_init_dec inv_mix_col transformation: direct inverse cipher
  requires the forward key schedule, not the equivalent-inverse schedule
- Remove unused inv_mix_col helper
- Correct HMAC-SHA256 RFC 4231 TC2 expected value (was wrong in test;
  implementation was always correct per openssl verification)
- Correct PBKDF2-SHA256 BasicVector expected value (was wrong in test;
  implementation matches hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac reference)

All 56 tests now pass (19 config + 19 crypto + 18 luks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor PresentPixmap to use struct-based options

Refactored PresentPixmap from 11 positional parameters to a single
PixmapPresentOptions struct with well-documented fields. This improves:

- Call-site readability: No more confusion about parameter order
- Future extensibility: Easy to add new optional fields
- Type safety: Named fields prevent parameter swapping errors

Changes:
- Added PixmapPresentOptions struct with 13 documented fields
- Updated PresentPixmap signature to accept single options parameter
- Updated all call sites (3 locations):
  - cmd/x11-dmabuf-demo/main.go
  - cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go
  - internal/x11/present/present_test.go (2 test cases)
- Simplified call sites using zero values for unused fields

Tests: All tests pass (make test-go)
Build: Verified with make build

Resolves AUDIT.md MEDIUM priority finding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor DecodeSetupReply to reduce complexity

Extract nested loops into helper functions for better testability:
- decodeSetupFailure: handles failure response path
- decodePixmapFormats: decodes pixmap format entries
- decodeVisuals: decodes visual entries for a depth
- decodeDepths: decodes depth entries for a screen
- decodeScreen: decodes a single screen with all sub-structures

Metrics improvement:
- Cyclomatic complexity: 11 → 8 (27.3% improvement)
- Overall complexity: 15.8 → 11.4 (27.8% improvement)
- Function length: 127 lines → 60 lines (main function)

All tests pass. No regressions detected.

Resolves: AUDIT.md finding "Long Function: DecodeSetupReply"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace bot with Nuxt 3 web app (Telegram Mini App + WebView)

Replaces the old Netlify-based Telegram bot with a full Nuxt 3 web
application that serves as both the Telegram Mini App (opened by the
bot) and the target URL for the Flutter mobile WebView shell.

Stack: Nuxt 3 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn-vue + Pinia + Axios
       + @twa-dev/sdk + vue-leaflet

Features:
- Auth: login, register, email verification
- Events: list, detail, join/leave
- Map: alumni map with city markers (vue-leaflet)
- Profile: view and edit (avatar, bio, location, Telegram alias)

Deployment:
- Docker: Node.js server on port 3000 (same pattern as frontend)
- docker-stack.yml: service 'mobile' on iu_alumni_network (replaces
  Flutter web deployment at mobile.*.escalopa.com)
- GitHub Actions CI/CD: PR to main → testing, push to main → production

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- setupExtensions(): DRI3 and Present extension setup
- setupGPU(): GPU detection and context creation
- setupX11Window(): Window creation and configuration

- Overall complexity: 14.8 → 7 (52.7% improvement)
- All tests passing (make test-go)
- Binary builds successfully (make gpu-triangle-demo)

commit.message:
fix: split PGLITE_CHUNK_IDS=100 / PGLITE_CHUNK_CONTENT=25

The WASM stack overflow is triggered by result DATA SIZE, not just
parameter count. 200 params × ~946 chars/atom = ~189KB through WASM
stack — causes __emscripten_memcpy_js overflow and corrupts PGlite state
(all subsequent queries fail, engine crashes).

Two-tier chunking:
- PGLITE_CHUNK_CONTENT = 25  (content queries: ~25KB/batch, safe margin)
- PGLITE_CHUNK_IDS     = 100 (ID/tag-only queries: small result rows)

fetchNodes now uses separate loops: content at chunk=25, tags at chunk=100.
bfsViaEdges and bfsViaTags frontier chunked at 100 (ID-only results).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: robust session recovery when copilot server evicts idle sessions

When the copilot server evicts an idle session (~30 min timeout),
SendPrompt now has a 3-phase recovery:

1. SDK resume (fast path) — works if session state is still on disk
2. Retry the prompt — catches the case where resume 'succeeds' but
   the server-side session is actually gone
3. Rebuild with history — creates a brand-new session seeded with
   the full conversation history, like a transparent fork

The user's message goes through on the rebuilt session with no error
state. The conversation continues seamlessly.

Previously, phase 2 would fail with the same 'Session not found' and
the session would enter a permanent error state with no way to recover.

Root cause: the copilot server evicted session f61cef46 after ~40 min
idle, SDK ResumeSession returned the same dead session ID, and the
retry loop never fell through to a rebuild.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add auto-copy share URL, pawn promotion dialog, rewrite README

Three improvements based on HN feedback (item 46580506):

1. Auto-copy share URL after each move: When a player makes a move,
   the share link is automatically copied to their clipboard and a
   brief toast notification confirms it. No more navigating to the
   Share tab just to grab the link.

2. Pawn promotion dialog: Previously all pawn promotions silently
   defaulted to queen. Now a dialog appears letting the player choose
   queen, rook, bishop, or knight.

3. README rewrite: Replaced the AI-generated README with something
   shorter and more direct. Removed the emoji-heavy formatting and
   repetitive feature list that multiple HN commenters flagged.
